description: A straightforward press/event photograph (not a designed meme) of a signing ceremony at ICE Headquarters. Two men in dark business suits sit at a table draped with a dark-blue cloth bearing a partially visible 'DEPARTMENT' seal, each signing documents in folders. On the left is Jeffrey DaRin (name placard: 'Jeffrey DaRin — HSI Deputy Assistant Director'); on the right is Felipe Tavares Seixas (placard: 'Felipe Tavares Seixas — Director of International Cooperation of The Brazilian Federal Police'). Small U.S. and Brazilian desk flags, microphones, and water bottles sit on the table; a full-size U.S. flag stands at left, and a projection screen behind reads 'Signing Ceremony for the Memorandum... United States Department of Hom[eland]... Immigration and Cust[oms]... Police of[ Brazil]' with U.S. and Brazilian flag graphics. The image is documentary photojournalism with no graphic-design overlay or meme styling. Per the tweet it documents ICE Homeland Security Investigations expanding a partnership with the Brazil Federal Police to combat transnational crime. Marked is_designed=false (routine candid event photo).
notable_text: Jeffrey DaRin — HSI Deputy Assistant Director; Felipe Tavares Seixas — Director of International Cooperation of The Brazilian Federal Police; Signing Ceremony for the Memorandum
